title: helleborus odorus subsp. cyclophyllus
intro: Helleborus odorus subsp. cyclophyllus, also known as the Christmas rose or bear's foot, is a flowering plant species within the Helleborus genus. Traditionally, this plant is used in herbal medicine, where its roots and leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, including their anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Helleborus odorus subsp. cyclophyllus include its ability to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Helleborus odorus subsp. cyclophyllus is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
